sanskrit: तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः । विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे राजन्  भगवान् श्रीकृष्णके उस अत्यन्त अद्भुत विराट्रूपको याद करकरके मेरेको बड़ा भारी आश्चर्य हो रहा है और मैं बारबार हर्षित हो रहा हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Remembering that astonishing form of Krishna again and again, I feel great wonder, O King, and I rejoice again and again.
essence: Remembering Krishna's vast form still overwhelms the mind with wonder.
speaker: Sanjaya
simpleMeaning: Sanjaya says that remembering Krishna's astonishing cosmic form fills him with wonder and repeated joy.
simpleInsight: A true glimpse of the divine leaves the mind stunned and the heart refreshed.
detailedMeaning: The war is over, but Sanjaya is still shaken. After recounting Krishna's teaching and Arjuna's vision, he pauses to say that remembering Krishna's cosmic form fills him with wonder and joy. The vision still lives in him.
modernRelevance: You replay a moment that changed you — a funeral, a birth, a breakup, a sudden act of grace — and it still stops you cold. Some experiences keep unfolding long after they end.
translationLiteral: तत् (that) / च (and) / संस्मृत्य (remembering) / संस्मृत्य (remembering again and again) / रूपम् (form) / अति-अद्भुतम् (extremely wondrous) / हरेः (of Krishna) / विस्मयः (wonder) / मे (my) / महान् (great) / राजन् (O king) / हृष्यामि (I rejoice) / च (and) / पुनः पुनः (again and again)
insight: It turns memory into reverence, showing that real vision keeps renewing awe.
